programmes.show_description: Young chamber musicians perform a selection of chamber works by Schubert in the concert hall of the Palau de la Música Catalana in Barcelona. Penny Gore presents.
Al Ryan presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Linton Stephens introduces an afternoon of specially made recordings, including a performance of Dvorák's Symphony No 9 from the Royal Scottish National Orchestra.

programmes.show_name: Composer of the Week: New Zealand Composers
programmes.show_description: 5/5. Donald Macleod explores five classical composers from the land of the long white cloud, New Zealand, focusing today on the life and music of Gareth Farr.
Petroc welcomes Lukas Sternath to the In Tune studio to perform ahead of his concert with the LPO at Brighton Dome. Also, The Solas Ensemble perform live from Edinburgh PQ.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music, including works by Delibes, Anna Meredith, Dvorák, Coleridge-Taylor, and Nino Rota amongst others.
Music and highlights from the annual Royal Philharmonic Society Awards given last night at the Queen Elizabeth Hall in London, introduced for Radio 3 by Sarah Walker.
What does a 1929 experimental silent documentary film sound like? Leo Geyer has been piecing together the score intended by director Dziga Vertov for his Man with a Movie Camera.
Verity Sharp shares a weave of music curated by Canadian Inuk artist-musician Tanya Tagaq.
Concert highlights from celebrated UK vocalist Heidi Vogel, recorded at Inntöne festival in Austria last summer.
Romanian pianist Andrei Licaret conducts and performs with the 'Magic Summer' Festival Orchestra in a concert from Bucharest featuring Beethoven's 2nd, 3rd and 4th piano concertos.
Hannah French presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Tom with an all star composer line up! Nico Muhly chats about his new album of choral music, plus Academy Award winner Hildur GuDnadóttir on some of her best-loved film scores.
Jools is joined by singer-songwriter Beth Rowley, with music by Brahms, Hildegard of Bingen and Ennio Morricone.
Violinist Tasmin Little looks at how, when and why musicians retire from performance. Featuring pianist Maria João Pires, violinist Lyn Fletcher and conductor Herbert Blomstedt.
Andrew McGregor with the best new classical releases, with guest reviewers Katy Hamilton and Nigel Simeone, who surveys recordings of Janacek's Sinfonietta and makes a top choice.
Edith Bowman presents an awards special, building up to Hollywood's biggest and most dazzling night with music from some of cinema's most celebrated and critically acclaimed scores.
Jess Gillam swaps music choices with the multi-award-winning opera and music theatre composer Conor Mitchell.
From the Metropolitan Opera in New York, Puccini's Madama Butterfly starring Aleksandra Kurzak and Adam Smith. Carlo Rizzi conducts.
Broadcaster, writer and DJ Zakia Sewell curates a rich selection of global roots-based music, exploring connections between identity, folk culture and myth.
Kate Molleson talks to the composer Rhodri Davies about the new music that he's discovering now, and Kate showcases the best and latest in cutting-edge and experimental new music.
